description:
Perform a Multiple Correspondence Analysis (MCA) on the tea consumption survey dataset.

Context: You are a Consumer Insights Researcher analyzing survey data to understand patterns in tea consumption behavior.

Task Requirements:
1. Install the 'FactoMineR' package and load the built-in 'tea' dataset (`data(tea)`).
2. Create a subset of the data using ONLY the first 18 columns (the active consumption variables).
3. Perform MCA on this subset.
4. Save the eigenvalues (percentage of variance) for the first 5 dimensions to '/home/ga/RProjects/output/mca_eigenvalues.csv'. Columns should include 'eigenvalue', 'variance_percent', 'cumulative_variance_percent'.
5. Save the variable category coordinates for the first 2 dimensions to '/home/ga/RProjects/output/mca_coordinates.csv'. Columns should include 'category', 'dim1', 'dim2'.
6. Create a biplot (perceptual map) of the variable categories and save it as '/home/ga/RProjects/output/mca_biplot.png'.
7. Identify which variable category has the HIGHEST contribution to Dimension 1 and write just the category name to '/home/ga/RProjects/output/mca_summary.txt'.

Write your analysis script in '/home/ga/RProjects/tea_analysis.R'.
